title: Understanding Angular Momentum Conservation
text: The angular momentum of the system is conserved because there are no external torques acting on it. This means that the initial angular momentum equals the final angular momentum.

title: Initial Angular Momentum
text: Calculate the initial angular momentum of the system. Use the formula for the moment of inertia of a disk and the angular velocity in radians per second.

title: Convert RPM to Radians per Second
text: Convert the initial angular velocity from rpm to radians per second. Use the conversion factor: $$1$$ rpm $$=$$ $$\frac{2\pi}{60}$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$.

title: Calculate Initial Moment of Inertia
text: Calculate the initial moment of inertia of the system. Consider the moment of inertia of the merry-go-round and the children at the edge.

title: Final Moment of Inertia
text: Calculate the final moment of inertia after the child moves to the center. Only the child with mass $$28$$ kg changes position.

title: Calculate Final Moment of Inertia
text: Calculate the final moment of inertia of the system. The child with mass $$28$$ kg is now at the center, so their contribution to the moment of inertia is zero.

title: Final Angular Velocity
text: Use the conservation of angular momentum to find the final angular velocity. Set the initial angular momentum equal to the final angular momentum.

title: Calculate Final Angular Velocity
text: Use the formula $$L_{initial}$$ $$=$$ $$L_{final}$$, where L $$=$$ $$I \omega$$. Solve for the final angular velocity.

title: Convert Final Angular Velocity to RPM
text: Convert the final angular velocity from radians per second back to rpm.
